mapQuestions.question: Which continent is South Africa on?
mapQuestions.answer: Africa. South Africa is at the very southern tip of the continent 🌍

mapQuestions.question: How many capital cities does South Africa have?
mapQuestions.answer: Three. Pretoria (executive — where the president lives), Cape Town (legislative — where parliament meets), and Bloemfontein (judicial — where the supreme court of appeal sits).

mapQuestions.question: Two oceans meet at the southern tip of South Africa. Which ones?
mapQuestions.answer: The Atlantic Ocean and the Indian Ocean. They officially meet at Cape Agulhas, the southernmost point of Africa 🌊

mapQuestions.question: How many official languages does South Africa have?
mapQuestions.answer: 11. English, Afrikaans, Zulu, Xhosa, Sotho, Tswana, Tsonga, Venda, Ndebele, Swazi, and Northern Sotho. Many South Africans speak three or more fluently.

mapQuestions.question: What two countries are completely surrounded by South Africa?
mapQuestions.answer: Lesotho (a mountain country in the middle of South Africa) and Eswatini (formerly called Swaziland, on the eastern edge). They are independent countries with their own kings.

foods.name: Braai
foods.description: Not just BBQ — a whole culture. Boerewors (spiced beef sausage), lamb chops, chicken thighs, all grilled slowly over wood embers. Best around a fire pit with friends.

foods.name: Bunny chow
foods.description: A hollowed-out half-loaf of white bread, filled with hot curry (often lamb, bean, or chicken). A Durban Indian invention, eaten with your hands.

foods.name: Bobotie
foods.description: Spiced minced beef baked with a savoury egg-and-milk topping, sometimes with raisins, almonds and apricot. South African comfort food, originally Cape Malay.

foods.name: Biltong
foods.description: Cured dried meat, vinegar-rubbed and air-dried for days into chewy salty strips. Eaten as snack food across the country. The South African version of jerky, but better.

foods.name: Koeksister
foods.description: A plaited or twisted dough deep-fried until golden and immediately soaked in cold sugar syrup. Cracks on the outside, sticky on the inside, intensely sweet.

foods.name: Rooibos tea
foods.description: A red-bush herbal tea grown only in the Cederberg mountains of South Africa. Caffeine-free, naturally sweet, drunk hot or iced. The country's most exported drink.

scavengerItems.label: A Big Five animal (elephant, lion, leopard, rhino or buffalo)
A zebra, giraffe, springbok or any other safari animal
Table Mountain seen from Cape Town
A "boerewors" sausage curling around itself on a braai grill
A wild penguin at Boulders Beach
A Nelson Mandela mural, statue or quote
A vineyard in the Cape Winelands
A real vuvuzela (long plastic horn from the 2010 World Cup)
Someone calling a traffic light a "robot"
A baobab tree with a fat round trunk
A traditional Zulu shield or Ndebele painted house pattern
Rooibos bushes growing wild in the Cederberg

geoMatches.description: On the south-western tip. Table Mountain rises straight out of the city, with cable cars to the top. Penguin colonies on white-sand beaches, wine country half an hour away.
"Jozi" — the biggest city, founded on a gold rush in 1886. Sprawling, energetic, and the home of the Apartheid Museum and Mandela's house in Soweto.
A safari park the size of Israel in the north-east. All of the Big Five live here, plus 500+ bird species. One of the best safari experiences in the world.
A massive escarpment of mountains running down eastern South Africa. The Zulu name is "uKhahlamba" — "the barrier of spears". UNESCO listed for its San rock art.
A 300 km stretch of coastline along the south coast. Forests, lagoons, white-sand beaches, the world's highest bungy jump from a bridge, and whales just offshore in season.
The Indian Ocean port city. The largest community of Indian people outside India, surf beaches, hot curries, and the home of bunny chow.

senses.smell: Boerewors smoking on a braai, fynbos plants in flower, dry savannah grass, rooibos brewing, Cape Town sea spray, Durban curry rising out of a kitchen…
senses.hear: The clicks of Zulu and Xhosa, "Howzit", "Just now", "Eish!", a lion roaring across the bush at night, vuvuzelas at a football match, surf on the Indian Ocean…
senses.taste: Sweet rooibos tea, smoky braai meat, hot spicy bunny chow, sweet sticky koeksister, salty dried biltong, the sharp sourness of amasi (fermented milk)…
senses.feel: Hot African sun, cold wind on top of Table Mountain, dry biltong texture on your fingers, sticky koeksister syrup, warm Indian Ocean water, soft Kalahari sand…
senses.see: What is the most incredible thing right in front of you right now…

stories.question: What is the "Big Five"?
stories.body: South Africa has some of the best safari in the world. The "Big Five" — elephant, lion, leopard, rhino and buffalo — all live in Kruger National Park, which is roughly the size of Israel. Other animals you can see: giraffes, zebras, hippos, cheetahs, wild dogs, baboons, and over 500 bird species. The "Big Five" got their name from big-game hunters who said they were the hardest and most dangerous animals to hunt on foot. Today they are the animals everyone wants to photograph. Rhinos are in deep trouble: poachers kill them for their horns, which are sold for more than gold in some Asian markets where people wrongly believe the horns have healing powers. South Africa has dedicated anti-poaching units that guard them around the clock.

stories.question: Why are there penguins in Africa?
stories.body: South Africa has wild penguins on a white-sand beach near Cape Town called Boulders Beach. They are African penguins, sometimes called "jackass" penguins because their call sounds exactly like a donkey braying. They live on a sandy beach you can walk on along a boardwalk, totally unbothered by humans. They were once everywhere along the southern African coast, but are now endangered: the population has dropped about 95% in 100 years from over-fishing, oil spills and climate change. The colony at Boulders started in the 1980s when two pairs of penguins moved in and somehow felt safe enough to stay. It is now home to around 3,000 birds.

stories.question: Why does South Africa have 11 languages?
stories.body: South Africa has 11 official languages: English, Afrikaans, Zulu, Xhosa, Sotho, Tswana, Tsonga, Venda, Ndebele, Swazi, and Northern Sotho. Many South Africans speak three or more fluently and switch between them mid-sentence. Zulu and Xhosa are particularly famous for "click" consonants — sharp pops made with the tongue against the roof of the mouth (try saying "Xhosa" properly: the X is a click). Nelson Mandela once said: "If you talk to a man in a language he understands, that goes to his head. If you talk to him in his language, that goes to his heart." Most South African TV news is read with subtitles, switching between two or three languages in a single bulletin.
stories.thinkingQuestion: When kids grow up speaking three or four languages, what do you think they understand about the world that other kids don't?

stories.question: What was apartheid?
stories.body: From 1948 to 1994, South Africa was ruled by a brutal system of legal racism called apartheid (literally "apartness" in Afrikaans). By law, white people (about 12% of the population) controlled the entire country. Black, mixed-race ("coloured"), and Indian South Africans had to carry passes, lived in separate areas, attended separate schools, used separate buses, beaches and water fountains, could not vote, and could not marry across "races". Nelson Mandela led the resistance. He spent 27 years in prison on Robben Island, off Cape Town. He was finally released in 1990. In 1994 he won South Africa's first free election and became the country's first Black president. Instead of seeking revenge, he set up a "Truth and Reconciliation Commission" where victims and perpetrators came forward in public to tell what had happened. Mandela died in 2013. His face is on every banknote.
stories.thinkingQuestion: Mandela chose forgiveness over revenge. What do you think that decision cost him personally? And what did it save?

stories.question: What is South Africa, 30 years after apartheid?
stories.body: When apartheid finally ended in 1994, many people predicted civil war. Instead, South Africa held the most-watched peaceful transition in modern history. People queued for hours, sometimes days, to vote for the first time. The new flag has six colours, the so-called "rainbow nation". But the work is not finished. South Africa still has one of the largest gaps between rich and poor on Earth. Unemployment is around 30%. Many Black South Africans still live in townships — poor areas physically separated from city centres by apartheid town planning — while wealthier areas (often, but not always, white) have private security, electric fences and gated communities. The country has made huge progress, but the legacy of 50 years of apartheid is still being worked through.

convoQuestions.question: Best South African food you have eaten?
Have you spotted a Big Five animal on safari yet? Which one was the wildest?
Have you heard a Zulu or Xhosa "click" in real conversation? Can you do one?
Cape Town, Joburg, Durban, the bush — which one has felt most "South African"?
Eleven languages, two oceans, three capitals, a million ways of being South African. What's been the most surprising thing?
Mandela chose forgiveness over revenge to keep the country together. Could that work in other places too?

wordSearch.hint: South African foods, places and things to spot.

animals.name: Plains zebra
animals.description: Black-and-white striped zebra in Kruger and most South African game parks.

animals.name: Giraffe
animals.description: Easy to spot above the bush in Kruger and many private reserves.

animals.name: African elephant
animals.description: Kruger has around 30,000. Often crossing the road in front of your car.

animals.name: Lion
animals.description: Best at dawn or dusk in Kruger, the Kalahari, or any of the big reserves.

animals.name: African penguin
animals.description: Boulders Beach near Cape Town. The only "jackass" penguins in Africa.

animals.name: Rhino
animals.description: Black or white rhino in Kruger and the Hluhluwe reserves. Guarded around the clock from poachers.

animals.name: Leopard
animals.description: Elusive but Sabi Sand has the highest sighting success in Africa.
